gold.description: Modern smartphones contain ~34 mg of gold across connectors and PCBs.

gold.displayName: ≈ the gold in an Olympic gold medal
gold.description: Olympic golds have been silver-and-gold-plated since the 1912 Stockholm games. Actual gold content: ~6 g.
400 troy oz LBMA-standard bar — the institutional unit of the gold market.
110.4 kg of solid gold. Egyptian Museum, Cairo.

gold.displayName: ≈ the largest gold bar ever cast

gold.description: Heathrow robbery, 2.7 tonnes of gold. Most never recovered.

gold.displayName: ≈ the Bank of England's gold reserves
gold.description: Post the 1999–2002 sale of ~395 tonnes at ~$275/oz — 'Brown's Bottom'.

gold.displayName: ≈ one year of global gold production

gold.description: Fort Knox, West Point Mint, Denver Mint combined.

gold.displayName: ≈ all gold in LBMA London vaults
gold.description: Roughly 8,500–9,200 tonnes across the LBMA vault network.
The largest private hoard on Earth — roughly 12% of all gold ever mined.
Fits in a cube ~22 m on a side. Warren Buffett's frame.

silver.displayName: ≈ the silver in one solar panel
silver.description: ~20 g per panel. Photovoltaic demand has been quietly reshaping the silver market for a decade.

silver.displayName: ≈ one LBMA silver Good Delivery bar
silver.description: 1,000 troy oz, the institutional silver wholesale unit.
1622 Spanish galleon, recovered from 1985. ~40 tonnes of silver bars and coins.
100 million troy oz directly held (~3,110 tonnes). Drove silver from $6 to $50/oz before Silver Thursday wiped them out.
130 million troy oz (~4,040 tonnes). Buffett's only significant commodity speculation, liquidated by 2006.

silver.displayName: ≈ all silver from the Comstock Lode
silver.description: 1859–1890s total output. Helped finance the Union in the Civil War.

silver.displayName: ≈ one year of global silver production

silver.description: All silver mined throughout human history.
